area_anchor: Brikette — When email sync fails, there's no way to tell which step went wrong
current_truth: Sync pipeline error logs include only thread ID and message with no step context. Metadata parse errors are silently swallowed returning empty objects. Telemetry write failures in recovery are silently ignored. Operators debugging failed syncs have no breadcrumbs.
next_scope_now: Add structured step-name logging to sync errors, log warnings for metadata parse failures, and log telemetry write failures.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: error logs lack step context; metadata parse errors silently swallowed; telemetry failures silent
why: When the email sync system has a problem, the error messages only say which email thread failed — not which step (fetching from Gmail, classifying, generating a draft, or saving to the database). Operators waste time guessing where things went wrong. Adding step names to error logs and surfacing previously silent failures makes debugging much faster.

intended_outcome.statement: All sync/recovery errors include the step name where failure occurred, metadata parse failures are logged as warnings, and telemetry write failures are logged.
